case.title: The Karaoke Killer
case.subtitle: A smoky bar-floor murder where a poisoned drink hides behind stage drama.
case.setting: The Velvet Room, Baton Rouge, Louisiana

finalQuestions.label: Why did the killer do it?
options: To hide staff theft Ricky discovered
To stop Ricky from exposing stolen lyrics
To retaliate for a personal betrayal

finalQuestions.label: How was the murder committed?
options: Ricky was struck during a backstage fight
Ricky was poisoned through his drink and then collapsed
Ricky was strangled behind the curtain

finalQuestions.label: Which clue most strongly supports the killer’s connection to the method?
options: Pill bottle cap in Selena’s area

hints.1: Look for the clue that changes the cause of collapse, not the clue that only proves tension.
hints.2: Ask which suspect is least protected by timestamps or staff observation.
hints.3: Who was dressed like a performer and not clearly accounted for elsewhere?
hints.4: If the fall wasn’t first, something else had to make Ricky lose control.
hints.5: Who had the most to lose from Ricky speaking publicly before the night ended?

suspects.name: Selena March
suspects.relationship: Promoted by Ricky, recently threatened by him
suspects.status_late: Strong motive, weak timeline, high opportunity

suspects.name: Mia Monroe
suspects.status_late: Deceptive, but likely not the killer

evidence_whiskey_glass.title: Ricky’s Whiskey Glass
evidence_whiskey_glass.desc: Half-finished whiskey with cloudy residue and lipstick on the rim.
evidence_whiskey_glass.short: Cloudy residue in Ricky’s drink suggests contamination.

evidence_lyric_fragment.title: Torn Lyric Fragment
evidence_lyric_fragment.desc: Fresh handwritten lyric scrap found near Ricky’s shoe behind the curtain.
evidence_lyric_fragment.short: Fresh lyric scrap ties the scene to written material.

evidence_bartender_statement.title: Bartender Statement
evidence_bartender_statement.desc: Tasha heard Ricky tell someone, ‘You’re done after tonight.’
evidence_bartender_statement.short: Ricky threatened someone before the finals.

evidence_security_note.title: Security Time Note
evidence_security_note.desc: Jax was outside and Damien was tied to booth activity during part of the window.
evidence_security_note.short: Two obvious suspects gain timing protection.

evidence_dress_sighting.title: Corridor Dress Sighting
evidence_dress_sighting.desc: A woman in dark stagewear slipped backstage after the argument.
evidence_dress_sighting.short: A performer-shaped figure had backstage access.

evidence_toxicology_flag.title: Preliminary Toxicology Flag
evidence_toxicology_flag.desc: Ricky likely began collapsing before the head strike.
evidence_toxicology_flag.short: The fall followed impairment, not the other way around.

evidence_pocket_note.title: Torn Pocket Note
evidence_pocket_note.desc: ‘Meet me after second set. I found proof.’
evidence_pocket_note.short: Ricky was expecting a private proof conversation.

evidence_bloody_curtain_smear.title: Bloody Curtain Smear
evidence_bloody_curtain_smear.desc: Small blood trace on a curtain tie near broken stemware.
evidence_bloody_curtain_smear.short: Someone besides Ricky bled backstage earlier.

evidence_broken_flute.title: Broken Champagne Flute
evidence_broken_flute.desc: Fresh broken stemware near the backstage curtain.
evidence_broken_flute.short: Broken glass suggests conflict but not method.

evidence_register_timestamp.title: Register Timestamp
evidence_register_timestamp.desc: A front-lounge transaction tied to Mia’s station closed at 11:51 PM.
evidence_register_timestamp.short: Mia was likely out front during the key window.

evidence_audio_booth_ping.title: Audio Booth Ping
evidence_audio_booth_ping.desc: Damien appears tied to booth activity around 11:53 PM.
evidence_audio_booth_ping.short: Damien was not as free as he first looked.

evidence_duplicate_lyric_page.title: Duplicate Lyric Page
evidence_duplicate_lyric_page.desc: Lyric material found with Selena closely matches the torn fragment.
evidence_duplicate_lyric_page.short: Selena possessed material matching the backstage clue.

evidence_pill_bottle_cap.title: Pill Bottle Cap
evidence_pill_bottle_cap.desc: Loose amber pill bottle cap found in Selena’s area.
evidence_pill_bottle_cap.short: Possible source container for what was added to Ricky’s drink.

evidence_unsent_voicemail.title: Unsent Voicemail
evidence_unsent_voicemail.desc: Ricky warned that someone had been stealing lyric material.
evidence_unsent_voicemail.short: Ricky feared retaliation over stolen lyrics.

evidence_setlist_scribble.title: Rehearsal Set List Scribble
evidence_setlist_scribble.desc: Selena’s prep timing appears adjusted to strengthen her alibi.
evidence_setlist_scribble.short: Selena’s timing may have been cleaned up in advance.

evidence_missing_cash_envelope.title: Missing Cash Envelope
evidence_missing_cash_envelope.desc: Cash drop envelope briefly unaccounted for during the night.
evidence_missing_cash_envelope.short: Separate staff drama muddies the room.

public: Ricky is found collapsed behind the curtain.

scene_001_opening.title: The Velvet Room
scene_001_opening.text: The Velvet Room should have ended with applause. Instead, it ended with silence. Behind the curtain, Ricky Vale is dead, and the room already wants to call it a drunken backstage collapse.

scene_002_body_reveal.title: Behind the Curtain
scene_002_body_reveal.text: Ricky lies between a backstage mirror and a rolling sound case. The story wants to be simple. The body does not.

scene_003_whiskey_glass.title: Ricky’s Drink
scene_003_whiskey_glass.text: The whiskey glass near Ricky carries cloudy residue and lipstick on the rim. The liquid matters more than the glamour around it.

scene_004_lyric_fragment.title: Torn Paper
scene_004_lyric_fragment.text: A fresh handwritten lyric scrap rests near Ricky’s shoe. It looks less like litter and more like motive.

scene_005_broken_glass.title: Shattered Stemware
scene_005_broken_glass.text: Broken champagne glass and a small blood trace suggest conflict backstage — but conflict is not the same thing as murder method.

scene_006_suspect_board_intro.title: Who Had Reason?
scene_006_suspect_board_intro.text: Four people rise quickly: a polished finalist, a furious rival, an ex who knows Ricky too well, and a sound tech tired of blame. In a room like this, motive is easy. Truth is not.

scene_007_round1_checkpoint.title: First Impression
scene_007_round1_checkpoint.prompt: You’ve seen the scene. One detail suggests Ricky’s death may have started before the fall. Which detail matters most?
answers: The cloudy residue in Ricky’s whiskey
The crowd still being in the room
scene_007_round1_checkpoint.correct: The whiskey changes the bigger question: whether Ricky collapsed because he drank something he was never supposed to drink.
scene_007_round1_checkpoint.incorrect: That clue may matter later, but it does not explain why Ricky lost control of his body.

scene_008_tasha_intro.title: Tasha Bell
scene_008_tasha_intro.text: The bartender has the look of someone who knows a room can lie faster than any person in it.

scene_009_leon_intro.title: Leon Guidry
scene_009_leon_intro.text: The door man remembers movement better than drama.

scene_010_selena_intro.title: Selena March
scene_010_selena_intro.text: Selena sits upright like posture itself is a defense.

scene_011_jax_intro.title: Jax Turner
scene_011_jax_intro.text: Jax looks exactly like the kind of man a room would blame first.

scene_012_mia_intro.title: Mia Monroe
scene_012_mia_intro.text: Mia looks less shocked than exhausted by the idea of Ricky even now.

scene_013_damien_intro.title: Damien Cross
scene_013_damien_intro.text: Damien answers questions like every extra word costs him something.

scene_014_round2_checkpoint.title: Whose Story Weakened?
scene_014_round2_checkpoint.prompt: After the first witness interviews, whose alibi weakens the most?
scene_014_round2_checkpoint.correct: Tasha’s account and Selena’s timeline do not sit cleanly together. Selena wants Ricky to seem fine later than the room comfortably supports.
scene_014_round2_checkpoint.incorrect: That suspect may still matter, but the strongest crack is where calm storytelling starts conflicting with witness timing.

scene_015_carla_intro.title: Carla Nguyen
scene_015_carla_intro.text: Carla saw movement in the side corridor that stopped feeling small once the body was found.

scene_016_register_log.title: Register Timestamp
scene_016_register_log.text: A front register entry at 11:51 PM gives Mia cleaner timeline footing than her emotions do.

scene_017_booth_ping.title: Booth Activity
scene_017_booth_ping.text: A booth-related ping ties Damien to audio trouble around 11:53 PM, narrowing his freedom during the likely poison window.

scene_018_mia_followup.title: Mia Revisited
scene_018_mia_followup.text: Mia finally admits Ricky thought he had proof someone in the scene was stealing lyric material. Some of it may have been hers.

scene_019_round3_checkpoint.title: Opportunity Window
scene_019_round3_checkpoint.prompt: Which suspect had the clearest unaccounted opportunity to tamper with Ricky’s drink?
scene_019_round3_checkpoint.correct: Jax, Mia, and Damien all gain some timing protection. Selena is the one still left standing in the gap.
scene_019_round3_checkpoint.incorrect: That suspect may feel noisy, but the cleanest access path belongs to the performer-shaped figure with the weakest outside support.

scene_020_dr_price_intro.title: Dr. Evan Price
scene_020_dr_price_intro.text: The coroner liaison is wonderfully uninterested in drama.

scene_021_dressing_search.title: Dressing Area Search
scene_021_dressing_search.text: In Selena’s area, lyric material echoes the torn backstage fragment. Deeper in the chaos sits a loose amber pill bottle cap.

scene_022_selena_followup.title: Selena Pressed
scene_022_selena_followup.text: Selena keeps calling patterns coincidence, but coincidence is starting to look expensive.

scene_023_round4_checkpoint.title: Method Theory
scene_023_round4_checkpoint.prompt: What theory now best fits Ricky Vale’s death?
answers: He was shoved into equipment during a fight
He was poisoned through his drink, then fell and struck equipment
He overdosed accidentally from drinking too much
scene_023_round4_checkpoint.correct: The head injury explains visible damage. The drink and medical sequence explain why he collapsed in the first place.
scene_023_round4_checkpoint.incorrect: That theory explains part of the scene, but not the full order of events.

scene_024_phone_review.title: Ricky’s Phone
scene_024_phone_review.text: Ricky left an unsent recording warning that if anything happened, investigators should check the lyric books.

scene_025_setlist_scribble.title: Rehearsal Notes
scene_025_setlist_scribble.text: A scribbled timing adjustment makes Selena’s prep story feel less like memory and more like maintenance.

scene_026_case_synthesis.title: What the Night Really Was
scene_026_case_synthesis.text: This case is no longer about who disliked Ricky. It is about sequence, contamination, access, and why Selena had to keep him from finishing the night.

scene_027_round5_checkpoint.title: Why Tonight?
scene_027_round5_checkpoint.prompt: Why was Ricky killed that night, before the show ended?
answers: He was about to expose Selena’s lyric theft publicly
He planned to get back together with Mia
He was taking Jax out of the finals for good
scene_027_round5_checkpoint.correct: Plenty of people disliked Ricky. Selena had the strongest reason to silence him before the night was over.
scene_027_round5_checkpoint.incorrect: That motive may explain tension, but not the urgency.

scene_028_final_accusation.title: Name the Killer
scene_028_final_accusation.text: The music is off. The glasses are still. Now the room wants a name.

ending_perfect.title: Perfect Solve
ending_perfect.text: You saw through the performance. Selena poisoned Ricky before the fall and acted in quiet panic because he had enough to expose what she built her future on. And in Ricky’s notes, the black treble-clef symbol appears again.

ending_solved.title: Case Solved
ending_solved.text: You got to the truth. Selena poisoned Ricky before the fall, and the lyric theft explains why she could not let him finish the night.

ending_partial.title: Close, But Incomplete
ending_partial.text: You found pieces of the truth, but not tightly enough to close every opening the room still offered.

ending_failed.title: Wrong Suspect
ending_failed.text: You followed the loudest version of the night. The real clue was waiting at the bottom of a drink.

interview_tasha.name: Tasha Bell
interview_tasha.intro: Ricky wasn’t stumbling around like a man about to drop dead. Let’s start there.

topics.label: Ricky’s Last Drink
topics.response: Whiskey. Straight. He was still moving like himself when it went back to him.

topics.label: Ricky’s Condition
topics.response: Buzzed? Sure. But not ‘body suddenly quits’ drunk.

topics.label: The Argument
topics.response: I heard Ricky say, ‘You’re done after tonight.’ That part was crystal clear.

topics.label: Who Was Nearby
topics.response: Selena kept orbiting that side more than once, and Jax looked like a wall deserved punishment.

interview_leon.name: Leon Guidry
interview_leon.intro: Everybody wants exact times after a body drops. Funny how that works.

topics.label: Jax Outside
topics.response: He stepped out angry and came back still carrying it.

topics.label: Time Window
topics.response: Around 11:49 when he went out, give or take.

topics.label: Damien Cross
topics.response: Booth side around 11:53. Not glued there every second, but close enough.

topics.label: Backstage Visibility
topics.response: That side corridor eats details. Once someone slips through the right angle, the room stops seeing them.

interview_selena.name: Selena March
interview_selena.intro: I’m cooperating. I’d just appreciate it if everyone stopped acting like being composed is suspicious.

topics.label: Ricky Argument
topics.response: Ricky liked to posture. He talked to people like their futures sat in his pocket.

topics.label: Last Time You Saw Ricky
topics.response: Near the mirror side. He was upright, talking, fully capable of being rude.

topics.label: Finals Status
topics.response: If Ricky threatened me, it was because he enjoyed making people feel unstable right before they needed to perform.

topics.label: Lyric Ownership
topics.response: People repeat phrases. They borrow emotion. Nobody owns heartbreak.

interview_jax.name: Jax Turner
interview_jax.intro: If you want the truth, fine: Ricky was a snake.

topics.label: Ricky Rivalry
topics.response: He played favorites and called it taste.

topics.label: Bloody Hand
topics.response: I hit something that didn’t hit back. Still not murder.

topics.label: Smoking Break
topics.response: Yeah, I went outside. Needed air.

topics.label: Selena March
topics.response: He was harder on her because he had more invested in her.

interview_mia.name: Mia Monroe
interview_mia.intro: Don’t do the thing where being the ex turns me into poetry in a police report.

topics.label: Money Ricky Owed
topics.response: Enough to irritate me. Not enough to make me sloppy.

topics.label: Relationship History
topics.response: Too much history, not enough trust, and a job that never let either of us disappear.

topics.label: Front Lounge Work
topics.response: Working. Drinks. Tabs. Same as always.

topics.label: Lyric Rumors
topics.response: Ricky hinted somebody was lifting material around this scene.

interview_damien.name: Damien Cross
interview_damien.intro: Ask what you want. Just ask it fast.

topics.label: Work Conflict
topics.response: Bad enough that I wouldn’t miss him professionally. Not bad enough to poison him over it.

topics.label: Booth Timing
topics.response: There was a monitor issue and I dealt with it.

topics.label: Backstage Access
topics.response: Everyone in staff has backstage access. That doesn’t make access the same as opportunity.

topics.label: Missing Cash Envelope
topics.response: If it was actually missing, management cared more tomorrow than tonight.

interview_carla.name: Carla Nguyen
interview_carla.intro: I didn’t see a face, so I don’t want my memory promoted into certainty.

topics.label: What You Saw
topics.response: A woman moving toward the backstage curtain from the side corridor. Quick. Head down.

topics.label: Clothing
topics.response: Dark dress. Stagewear, not service blacks.

topics.label: Could It Be Mia?
topics.response: Not unless Mia suddenly dressed for the finals without telling anyone.

topics.label: Time of Sighting
topics.response: After voices got tense backstage. Before the room fully realized something was wrong.

interview_price.name: Dr. Evan Price
interview_price.intro: You already have enough noise in this case. I’ll keep to what the body is willing to say.

topics.label: Cause Sequence
topics.response: The collapse came first. The head injury is more consistent with secondary impact than primary attack.

topics.label: Poisoning Theory
topics.response: ‘Simple drunken fall’ is not a satisfying explanation.

topics.label: Alcohol Alone
topics.response: Alcohol may be present. That is not the same as alcohol being sufficient.

topics.label: Time to Collapse
topics.response: Fast enough that the fall could appear accidental to bystanders in a loud room.
